               <jats:p>The viability of using principal component analysis (PCA) and soft independent modeling of class analogy analysis (SIMCA) of the near-infrared reflectance spectra of illicit methaqualone tablet formulations as an aid in sample differentiation was investigated.</jats:p>
               <jats:p>Near-infrared spectra of ten different illicit species occurring on the South African market was obtained through direct diffuse reflectance measurements made on the tablet surfaces using a bidirectional fiber-bundle probe.</jats:p>
               <jats:p>The learning set was analyzed using PCA and SIMCA and resolved in well-separated clusters thus making differentiation possible.</jats:p>","DOI":"10.1520/jfs13879j","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2015,8,14]],"date-time":"2015-08-14T09:10:08Z","timestamp":1439543408000},"page":"1072-1076","update-policy":"https://doi.org/10.1115/crossmarkpolicy-asme","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":2,"title":["The Differentiation of Illicit Methaqualone Tablet Formulations Using Principal Component and Soft Independent Modeling of Class Analogy Analysis of Their Near-Infrared Reflectance Spectra"],"prefix":"10.1520","volume":"40","author":[{"given":"EF","family":"Van Zyl","sequence":"first","affiliation":[{"name":"Forensic Science Laboratory 1 Forensic Analysts , , Private Bag X620 Pretoria0001Republic of South Africa"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"M","family":"Louw","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[{"name":"Forensic Science Laboratory 1 Forensic Analysts , , Private Bag X620 Pretoria0001Republic of South Africa"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"381","reference":[{"issue":"4","key":"2025063018094734900_c1","doi-asserted-by":"crossref","first-page":"651","DOI":"10.1520/JFS11418J","article-title":"Approaches to Drug Sample Differentiation.
